Home.lead: Adaptive exercises for differentiation. Start immediately, get instant feedback, and for every mistake the site explains exactly what went wrong — with a follow-up question tailored to that mistake.
Home.subLead: There are two ways to practice: a {leerpad} that adapts to how you answer questions, and {vrijOefenen} where you choose topics yourself. On your first visit you can indicate what you already know or take a short diagnostic test. {howItWorks}.
Home.feature1Body: No long explanatory videos. You learn derivatives by working through them and getting explanations for every mistake.
Home.feature2Body: The site tracks your pace. You get more of what you find difficult, less of what you already know.
Home.feature3Body: Notation and learning progression follow a logical order. From the power rule to the chain rule.
Home.topicsIntro: All differentiation rules for high school calculus, in increasing difficulty:
Home.topic1Body: The foundation. From xⁿ to n·xⁿ⁻¹, including negative and fractional exponents.
Home.topic3Body: For functions like (x² + 1)·sin(x). Two parts each separately, then combine.
Home.topic4Body: For fractions like (x² + 1) / (x − 3). Only when the product rule is not easier.
Home.topic5Body: For composite functions like sin(3x²). Bring in the inner function's derivative.
Home.topic6Body: sin, cos, ln, e, and rational functions — having these ready is half the battle.
Home.how1Body: For example: find the derivative of f(x) = 3x² + 2x − 5. You type your answer using a math keyboard.
Home.how2Body: Correct? On to the next one. Wrong? The site explains which rule you should have used, works through the steps, and shows exactly where your answer went wrong.
Home.how3Body: Based on your mistake you get an exercise that addresses exactly that type of problem. So you fix the gap instead of ignoring it.
Home.how4Body: When you answer a topic correctly several times in a row, the site marks it as mastered. You'll see it less often after that.
Home.ctaBody: Create a free account and practice your first fifty derivatives today. No credit card. No limit.
Home.faq1A: Yes, completely free. Create an account and start practising immediately. No ads, no trial subscription.
Home.faq2A: For students who need to practise differentiation rules. The site covers the power rule, sum rule, product rule, quotient rule, chain rule, and standard derivatives (sin, cos, ln, e).
Home.faq3A: All differentiation rules for high school calculus: power rule, sum and difference rule, constant factor rule, product rule, quotient rule, and chain rule. Plus standard derivatives of sine, cosine, exponential and logarithmic functions.
Home.faq4Q: What is the difference between the learning path and free practice?
Home.faq4A: The learning path guides you adaptively: based on your answers you get more of what you find difficult and less of what you already master. Free practice is your choice: pick topics yourself and practise without a set order. More explanation is on the How it works page.
Home.faq5Q: How does the adaptive approach work?
Home.faq5A: The site tracks per topic how well you master it. Exercises you find difficult appear more often; topics you already know appear less often. For every mistake the site explains exactly what went wrong and gives you a fitting follow-up question to fix it.
Home.faq6Q: Is this suitable for exam preparation?
Home.faq6A: Absolutely. Derivatives are the foundation for large parts of the calculus exam (extrema, tangent lines, dynamic models). By becoming fluent here you'll work through the other parts of the exam faster and with fewer mistakes.
Home.faq7Q: Is this only for Dutch students?
Home.faq7A: The exercises were originally written in Dutch, but the theory and all interface text are available in English. The mathematical content is of course universal.

HoeHetWerkt.title: How it works · derivativepractice.nl
HoeHetWerkt.description: The difference between the learning path and free practice, how the site adapts, and how to set your preferences on the first visit.
HoeHetWerkt.intro: This site has one goal: to get you faster and stronger at differentiation, at your own level. That's why the site has two clear components — plus a short choice at the start about what suits you.
HoeHetWerkt.card1Body1: You follow the curriculum: from the power rule to sum rule, product rule, quotient rule, and chain rule.
HoeHetWerkt.card1Body2: the path automatically adapts to what you answer. Mistakes get you more explanation and fitting follow-up questions; what you already master you see less often — without having to plan everything upfront.
HoeHetWerkt.card2Body: Here you {strong} (power rule, chain rule, etc.). The same kind of questions as in the learning path, but without the site pushing you through a fixed order. Handy for revision, exam cramming, or just tackling one chapter.
HoeHetWerkt.card2BodyStrong: choose a topic yourself each time
HoeHetWerkt.firstTimeBody: After registration we ask a few short questions. First you choose whether to start with the {leerpad} or with {vrijOefenen}. If you choose the learning path, you can tune how the path should start: from step one, with a topic checklist, or with a short test. If you choose free practice, you go straight to the practice page to pick topics — the learning path remains accessible in the menu.
HoeHetWerkt.option1Body: we walk through the learning path with you from the beginning.
HoeHetWerkt.option2Title: I know what I want to practise
HoeHetWerkt.option2Body: tick which topics you already know and which you want to train; the learning path will match that.
HoeHetWerkt.option3Body: a few questions to estimate where you start; then you continue on the learning path from there.
HoeHetWerkt.option4Title: I choose my own topics each time
HoeHetWerkt.option4Body: you land at {vrijOefenen}; the learning path remains available for later in the menu.
HoeHetWerkt.summary1Adapts: adapts to your mistakes and progress
HoeHetWerkt.summary2: {free} = you open a topic yourself each visit; works great alongside the learning path for revision.
HoeHetWerkt.summary3: Both use the {same} and the same account — your dashboard and progress are in sync.

Theorie.title: Differentiation — all rules on one page · derivativepractice.nl
Theorie.description: All differentiation rules for high school calculus on one page: power rule, sum rule, product rule, quotient rule, chain rule, exponential, trigonometry and ln. With examples and steps.
Theorie.h1: Differentiation — all rules on one page
Theorie.intro: A reference for all the differentiation rules you'll encounter in calculus. Per topic you see the formula, a worked example and — when you expand it — explanation and steps. You can also go straight to {practiceLink}.
Theorie.noTheory: No theory for this cluster yet — exercises only.

Login.subtitle: Welcome back. Log in to continue practising.

ForgotPassword.title: Forgot password · derivativepractice.nl

ForgotPassword.sentMessage: If this email belongs to an account, you'll receive a reset link shortly. Check your spam folder too.

Onboarding.title: Welcome · derivativepractice.nl
Onboarding.step1Intro: This helps us tailor the content to your level.
Onboarding.step2Intro: Just your first name, to address you personally. We won't fill this in from your email — your choice.
Onboarding.step3Intro: There are two ways: the {leerpad} guides you adaptively through the topics (following the curriculum) — handy if you want structure. With {vrijOefenen} you choose a topic yourself each session, without a fixed route — handy for revision or if you just want one chapter. You can always switch in the menu later.
Onboarding.card1Body: You follow the thread: power rule, sum rule, product rule, quotient rule, chain rule. The path automatically adapts to your answers: where you go wrong you get focused explanation and follow-up questions; what you master comes back less often.
Onboarding.card2Body: You choose which topic to open each time — the same kind of questions and feedback as in the learning path, but without a fixed order. You'll end up on the practice page to pick topics freely (the learning path remains accessible in the menu).
Onboarding.step4Intro: How do you want to start the path? You can always switch later or go to free practice.
Onboarding.guidedDesc: We start at the first rule and work step by step through the learning path.
Onboarding.topicSelectLabel: I know which topics I want to practise
Onboarding.topicSelectDesc: Tick what you already know. We'll put together a learning path tailored to you.
Onboarding.diagnosticDesc: 5 questions to estimate your level. Then your learning path starts at the right point.

OnboardingPad.title: Build learning path · derivativepractice.nl
OnboardingPad.h1: What do you already know, what do you want to practise?
OnboardingPad.intro: Tick which topics you already know well and which you want to practise. We'll build your learning path based on that.
OnboardingPad.errorMin: Choose at least one topic to practise.
OnboardingPad.skipHint: the default learning path stays as it is

OnboardingToets.title: Short test · derivativepractice.nl
OnboardingToets.h1: This is how we estimate your starting point
OnboardingToets.intro: One question per main topic. Based on your answers we'll set up your learning path.
OnboardingToets.noQuestions: No suitable questions for the test yet.
OnboardingToets.resultsIntro: Here you can see per question whether your answer was correct or incorrect.
OnboardingToets.padIntro: Based on the test we suggest what you already know and what you want to practise. You can still adjust this.
OnboardingToets.errorMin: Choose at least one topic to practise.

Dashboard.modeFreeBody: The full learning path is still in the menu for a fixed route.

Leerpad.title: Learning path · derivativepractice.nl
Leerpad.completeBody: All topics and clusters are completed. You can view your progress on your dashboard.
Leerpad.noQuestionsBody: You've answered all questions in this cluster correctly 3 times, but the cluster isn't finished yet. We'll generate the next round of exercises in Phase 3 via AI.
